narration: Every fall, your employer drops open enrollment in your lap. And 51% of workers quietly pick the wrong plan — costing themselves over $2,100 every single year.
visual: Medical cross SVG centered on dark background. Counter animates up to 51%. Text: OPEN ENROLLMENT at top. $2,100/YEAR MISTAKE pulses in red at bottom.

narration: Most people just re-enroll in last year's plan without looking. That's called status quo bias — and your HR department is counting on it.
visual: Clipboard SVG with two checkbox rows both showing 'Same Plan' checked. STATUS QUO BIAS label slides in. Circular arrow showing the repeating loop.

narration: The word 'deductible' scares almost everyone off the better plan. But if you're healthy, the high-deductible option almost always wins the actual math.
visual: Two plan cards: PPO at $320/mo with a red cost badge, HDHP at $160/mo with a fear X that animates into a green checkmark as narration progresses.

narration: Here's the secret weapon hiding in high-deductible plans: an HSA. It's the only account that's triple tax-free — contributions, growth, and withdrawals, all untaxed.
visual: Piggy bank SVG with a coin dropping in. Three blue label badges spring in one at a time: TAX-FREE IN, TAX-FREE GROWTH, TAX-FREE OUT.

narration: A typical worker on a standard plan pays $4,800 in premiums yearly. Switch to the HDHP at $2,400, invest that $2,400 gap in your HSA — over ten years, that's $34,000 built.
visual: Side-by-side animated bar chart: PPO bar grows to $4,800 in red, HDHP+HSA stacked bar shows $2,400 premium plus green HSA growth reaching $34,000 over 10 years.

narration: This open enrollment, run the real math — total premiums plus average out-of-pocket, not just the monthly number. Share this with someone picking a plan by feel.
visual: Calculator SVG with a three-item checklist appearing one by one: Total Premiums, Avg Out-of-Pocket, HSA Option. Blue checkmarks appear. Bottom CTA: SHARE BEFORE ENROLLMENT.
